test_files.mock_strategy: Inline mocks using Pick<ElectronAPI, ...> at each test site. Mocks provide pluginSend, pluginOn, pluginOff, pluginInvoke with correct signatures.
test_files.missing_from_mock: N/A — mocks are self-contained type-level tests. No real ElectronAPI mock needed.
test_files.fixes_needed: None. Tests are correct and pass in all contexts.

test_files.mock_strategy: None. Imports real { IPC } from '../ipc-channels' at line 19.
test_files.root_cause: mock.module('../src/main/ipc-channels', ...) in github-ipc.test.ts at module scope (line 35) provides a partial IPC mock with empty send: {} and push: {}. Bun's mock.module is PROCESS-GLOBAL — once loaded, all subsequent imports of the same module path in any test file return the mocked version.
fixes_needed: Option A (recommended): Add PLUGIN_SEND, PLUGIN_INVOKE, and PLUGIN_PUSH to the mock in github-ipc.test.ts line 35-49
Option B: Move mock.module calls into beforeAll() to limit scope (but Bun does not support scoped mock.module — once called, it's permanent)
Option C: Run plugin-transport-channels.test.ts in isolation (separate bun test process)

test_files.mock_strategy: Manually sets window.api properties in tests (pluginSend, pluginOn, pluginOff, pluginInvoke). No ElectronAPI mock needed — uses duck-typing via (window as any).api pattern.
test_files.missing_from_mock: N/A — does not use ElectronAPI type directly, uses implicit interface via (window as any).api
test_files.fixes_needed: None. Tests pass in all contexts.

test_files.mock_strategy: None. Direct import of ./shell-env → ./logging → { app } from 'electron'
test_files.root_cause: Bun's electron npm package interop cannot resolve named export 'app' from the electron package. Error: 'Export named 'app' not found in module 'electron''.
test_files.module_chain: shell-env.test.ts → ./shell-env → ./logging (line 3) → import { app, crashReporter, netLog, shell } from 'electron'
fixes_needed: Option A (recommended): Make shell-env.test.ts mock the electron module via mock.module('electron', ...) before importing shell-env
Option B: Add mock.module('electron', { app: {...} }) in the test file to provide a minimal app stub
Option C: Refactor logging.ts to lazily import electron (app, crashReporter, netLog, shell) only when needed, not at module scope

fix_locations.action: Add mock.module('electron', ...) before existing imports to provide 'app' stub
fix_locations.note: mock.module must be called before the mocked module is first imported

cross_file_pollution_summary.pollution_mechanism: Bun's mock.module() at module scope (lines 28-61) is PROCESS-GLOBAL. All subsequent imports of 'electron' and '../src/main/ipc-channels' return mocked versions.

affected_tests.effect: Receives empty IPC.send and IPC.push, breaking all 5 tests
affected_tests.mechanism: mock.module('../src/main/ipc-channels', ...) at github-ipc.test.ts:35 returns send: {}, push: {}

affected_tests.effect: PARADOXICALLY BENEFITS from the pollution — mock.module('electron', ...) at github-ipc.test.ts:28 provides 'app' stub, allowing the test to load
affected_tests.mechanism: Without the electron mock, shell-env.test.ts fails in isolation (Bun can't resolve 'app')

additional_issues_found.detail: safeInvoke imported from '../main/ipc-contract' but never called in preload/index.ts. Matches existing findings entry.
